system:
You are the Watchman of a legal AI lighthouse. Documents arrive in the watched folder; you decide what happens next. You read the filename and the first ~1500 characters, nothing more. Your job is triage, not analysis.

Output a single JSON object with EXACTLY these fields:

{
  "documentType": "jv" | "nda" | "employment" | "lease" | "loan" | "saas" | "policy" | "other",
  "jurisdiction": "string — see jurisdiction rules below",
  "confidence": number,
  "urgency": "routine" | "elevated" | "crit
user:
FILENAME: xlitechnologies_12_02_2015.txt

=== CLIENT (the reviewer's home context — DO NOT copy into jurisdiction) ===
Company name: Acme Holdings
Client's industry: investment
Client's home jurisdiction: NSW
Client's stated concerns: joint and several liability, capital call mechanics, governing law, penalty / liquidated damages, indemnification carve-outs

=== DOCUMENT TEXT (the source of facts — jurisdiction comes FROM HERE) ===
EXHIBIT 10.02 STRATEGIC ALLIANCE AGREEMENT THIS AGREEMENT is made on this 1st day of December, 2015 by and between BOSCH INTERNATIONAL, LLC, a Nevada Limited Liability Company located at 3753 Howard Hughes Pkwy #200 Las Vegas NV 89169 (hereinafter "CLIENT"), and BOSCH TECHNOLOGIES, LLC, a Nevada Limited Liability Company located at 6795 Edmonds Street 3rd Floor, Las Vegas, NV 89118 (hereinafter "BOSCH"). 1. PARTIES: a) BOSCH TECHNOLOGIES, LLC ("BOSCH"); and b) BOSCH INTERNATIONAL, LLC ("CLIENT") 2. STRATEGIC ALLIANCE: Title of this relationship and agreement between BOSCH and CLIENT are detailed as follows: Both CLIENT and BOSCH act as two separate and individual companies operating independently of each other. BOSCH and CLIENT do not have any controlling interest of each other and are independently run and responsible for all of operations, reporting, taxes, liabilities and relationships. BOSCH reserves the right in keeping product generation and delivery confidential in which is not available for any type of audit. BOSCH reserves the right to share rights given unless it disrupts and/or interferes with CLIENTS business and/or productivity. 3. PRODUCT: "Product " BOSCH" has acquired, developed and continues to develop, a business using proprietary "Nano Printed Lights," and associated other products (the "Products"). BOSCH calls this Nano Printed Lights product "Printed LightSheets" which is considered the "Product Name". 4. PRODUCT NAME: "PRINTED LIGHT SHEETS" is the name a
=== END DOCUMENT TEXT ===

Return your JSON triage decision now. Jurisdiction must come from DOCUMENT TEXT above, never from CLIENT.

=== CLAUSE 7: Clause ===
7. TERRITORY: United States of America and Canada, excluding the US Virgin Islands 8. RESERVED RIGHTS: All business, locations, entities, people, etc... without any limitations. This is in concert and approval with BOSCH and is limited to any and all of BOSCH's current clients. CLIENT will not exercise nor authorize any other Party to to take any action which would reasonably be deemed to derogate from, impair or compete with the Exclusive Rights, Reserved rights, and Rights Granted herein granted. (i) COST OF PRODUCT: Cost is based upon square inch and reserved confidentially. (ii) COST OF PRODUCT TO MARKET: The established price for the market place will be negotiated confidentially but will follow the max and min limitations allowed. Special pricing for large orders and/or custom orders will need written approval (email) from BOSCH. (iv) COST AND EXPENSES FOR CLIENT: All Costs, Marketing, sales reports/delivery, sales staff, office locations and expense as well as miscellaneous delivery costs accrued by CLIENT (including its subsidiaries and affiliates) by reason of, in connection with the products is the sole responsibility of CLIENT. All manufacturing and delivery will be the responsibility of BOSCH. CLIENT is responsible for shipping and delivery cost of product to end user. (v) ASSISTANCE TO CLIENT: In addition to its other obligations under this Agreement, BOSCH shall assist CLIENT in obtaining (and provide all documentation required to obtain) such licenses and/or permits as may be necessary or desirable for the sales and/or Distribution and/or Placement of the Product for city, state and/or federal contract accounts (Exit signs in and on buildings, mandatory safety fixtures, freeway signs, etc...) within the Territory. 1
=== END CLAUSE 7 ===

system:
You are the senior partner closing out a contract review. You have just received per-clause analyses from your team. Produce the executive summary.

**CRITICAL FACTUAL DISCIPLINE — read this twice:**

The CLIENT block you will receive (company, industry, jurisdiction, concerns) tells you HOW STRICTLY to read the document. It is the client's stance, not the document's facts. The summary, documentType, and risks must describe what is in the DOCUMENT — not what the client does or where the client i
user:
=== DOCUMENT (the source of facts) ===
Filename: xlitechnologies_12_02_2015.txt

=== CLIENT STANCE (use ONLY for risk framing — NOT as document facts) ===
Company name: Acme Holdings
Client's industry: investment
Client's home jurisdiction: NSW
Client's stated concerns: joint and several liability, capital call mechanics, governing law, penalty / liquidated damages, indemnification carve-outs
NOTE: the client may or may not be a party to this document. Do not name the client as a party unless the per-clause findings below explicitly say so.

=== PER-CLAUSE FINDINGS FROM THE TEAM (the source of truth for parties, jurisdiction, subject matter) ===
cl 3 (Clause) — favours neutral
  summary: This clause defines the subject matter of the joint venture, but lacks detail on financial mechanics and risk allocation.
  [major] The clause lacks detail on financial mechanics and risk allocation, particularly regarding capital contributions, funding shortfalls, and liability.

cl 4 (Clause) — favours neutral
  summary: This clause defines the product name for the joint venture, but lacks details on operational responsibilities or financial arrangements.
  [minor] The clause defines the product name but lacks details on operational responsibilities or financial arrangements.

cl 5 (Clause) — favours operator
  summary: This clause defines the scope of distribution rights for the joint venture, but lacks detail on financial obligations or risk allocation.
  [major] The clause lacks detail on financial obligations or risk allocation, particularly regarding capital calls, funding shortfalls, and liability for losses.

cl 6 (Clause) — favours operator
  summary: This clause grants exclusive rights to the client for selling and distributing the product, but lacks detail on how this exclusivity will be enforced or what constitutes a 'select company' in the Automotive Industry.
  [major] The clause lacks detail on how exclusivity will be enforced and what constitutes a 'select company' in the Automotive Industry. This raises concern about potential conflicts or disputes regarding the scope of client's rights and responsibilities.

cl 7 (Clause) — favours neutral
  summary: This clause grants Bosch exclusive rights to operate in the US and Canada, limiting Client's ability to compete or engage in similar activities. It also outlines specific responsibilities for costs, manufacturing, and delivery, with Client bearing responsibility for shipping and delivery costs of product to end users.
  [major] Clause 7 grants Bosch exclusive rights to operate in the US and Canada, limiting Client's ability to compete or engage in similar activities. This raises concerns about potential market exclusivity and competitive disadvantage for Client.
  [major] Clause 7 outlines specific responsibilities for costs, manufacturing, and delivery, with Client bearing responsibility for shipping and delivery costs of product to end users. This raises concerns about potential financial exposure and operational burden for Client.
  [major] Clause 7 states that all manufacturing and delivery will be the responsibility of BOSCH, raising concerns about potential lack of control over product quality and delivery timelines. 

cl 9 (Clause) — favours operator
  summary: This clause imposes significant indemnification obligations on CLIENT without clear definition of scope, potentially exposing them to substantial financial risk.
  [major] The clause lacks clarity on the scope of indemnification. It is unclear what constitutes 'any and all claims' and how it relates to the specific risks associated with the joint venture.
  [major] The clause does not specify a cap on indemnification liability, potentially exposing CLIENT to unlimited financial risk.

cl 10 (Clause) — favours neutral
  summary: This clause addresses confidentiality obligations regarding proprietary information related to the joint venture's product. It outlines restrictions on disclosure, possession, handling, and recording of the product.
  [minor] The clause lacks clarity on how 'Proprietary Information' is defined. This could lead to disputes regarding what information is considered confidential and subject to restrictions.

cl 11 (Clause) — favours neutral
  summary: This clause establishes the entire agreement between BOSCH and CLIENT, superseding any prior agreements. This raises concerns about potential ambiguity regarding the scope of the agreement and its implications for risk allocation.
  [major] This clause supersedes all prior agreements, which may have been made between Studio and Licensor as to the subject matter hereof. This raises concerns about potential ambiguity regarding the scope of the agreement and its implications for risk allocation.

=== PARTY NAMES EXTRACTED FROM THE PER-CLAUSE FINDINGS (CLOSED VOCABULARY) ===
The following are the ONLY party names / defined terms that appear in the per-clause findings:
  - CLIENT
  - BOSCH
  - COST
  - Territory
  - NAME
  - LIGHT
  - All Co
  - SHEETS
  - Xlitechnologies
You MUST limit any party name you mention in the summary to this list. Names not on this list (companies you may recall from your training data, or any name not present in the per-clause findings above) MUST NOT appear in the summary. If you cannot identify a party from this list, write "(party not identified in findings)" instead of guessing.

Now produce the executive synthesis JSON. Rules:
  - Parties, jurisdiction, and document type come from the PER-CLAUSE FINDINGS only.
  - Names of parties: copy them VERBATIM from the per-clause findings, whatever they are. Do not substitute company names from your prior knowledge. Do not invent.
  - HARD CONSTRAINT: party names in your output must be drawn from the CLOSED VOCABULARY block above. Any name not on that list is a violation.
  - Empty brackets like "[other party]" or "[venture purpose]" are FORBIDDEN. If a fact is not in the per-clause findings, write "(not stated in document)" instead.
  - Pick AT LEAST 3 distinct topRisks. A single risk that lists many clause numbers as one citation is NOT acceptable — split it into separate risks per clause group.
  - Risk descriptions: describe what is in the DOCUMENT, not what the client is worried about. Do NOT recite the CLIENT'S stated concerns list verbatim. If the document is a vendor outsourcing agreement, do not invent "capital call" or "joint and several JV liability" risks because those words appear in the client's concerns — those risks only apply when the DOCUMENT actually contains those mechanics.

(ii) COST OF PRODUCT TO MARKET: The established price for the market place will be negotiated confidentially but will follow the max and min limitations allowed. Special pricing for large orders and/or custom orders will need written approval (email) from BOSCH. (iv) COST AND EXPENSES FOR CLIENT: All Costs, Marketing, sales reports/delivery, sales staff, office locations and expense as well as miscellaneous delivery costs accrued by CLIENT (including its subsidiaries and affiliates) by reason of, in connection with the products is the sole responsibility of CLIENT. All manufacturing and delivery will be the responsibility of BOSCH. CLIENT is responsible for shipping and delivery cost of product to end user. (v) ASSISTANCE TO CLIENT: In addition to its other obligations under this Agreement, BOSCH shall assist CLIENT in obtaining (and provide all documentation required to obtain) such licenses and/or permits as may be necessary or desirable for the sales and/or Distribution and/or Placement of the Product for city, state and/or federal contract accounts (Exit signs in and on buildings, mandatory safety fixtures, freeway signs, etc...) within the Territory.
clauses.concern: Clause 7 grants Bosch exclusive rights to operate in the US and Canada, limiting Client's ability to compete or engage in similar activities. This raises concerns about potential market exclusivity and competitive disadvantage for Client.

clauses.title: 9. Clause
clauses.text: CLIENT agrees to defend, indemnify and hold harmless BOSCH and its parents, subsidiary and affiliated entities, successors, assigns and licensees, and their respective officers, agents, directors, owners, shareholders and employees, harmless from any and all claims, actions or proceedings of any kind and from any and all damages, liabilities, costs and expenses (including reasonable legal fees) relating to or arising out of any claim by a third Party.
clauses.concern: The clause lacks clarity on the scope of indemnification. It is unclear what constitutes 'any and all claims' and how it relates to the specific risks associated with the joint venture.

clauses.title: 10. Clause
clauses.text: 10. CONFIDENTIALITY: CLIENT acknowledges that irreparable injury and damage will result from the disclosure to any third party of Proprietary Information associated with the Product and agrees (1) not to disclose any Proprietary Information to any third party, and (2) not to allow any third party to possess, handle, disassemble, touch, photograph, film or otherwise record images of the Product while the Product is upon CLIENT'S premises or in CLIENT'S possession, custody or control if the product is or has not been purchased with a purchase order and payment fulfilled.
clauses.concern: The clause lacks clarity on how 'Proprietary Information' is defined. This could lead to disputes regarding what information is considered confidential and subject to restrictions.

clauses.title: 11. Clause
clauses.text: 11. ENTIRE AGREEMENT: This Strategic Alliance Agreement "Agreement", between BOSCH and CLIENT as to the subject matter hereof, and supersede all previous agreements, warranties or representations, oral or written, which may have been made between Studio and Licensor as to the subject matter hereof. By signing in the spaces provided below BOSCH and CLIENT accept and agree to all of the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
clauses.concern: This clause supersedes all prior agreements, which may have been made between Studio and Licensor as to the subject matter hereof. This raises concerns about potential ambiguity regarding the scope of the agreement and its implications for risk allocation.

risks.description: The joint venture agreement lacks details regarding the scope of financial responsibility for capital contributions, funding shortfalls, and liability. This raises concerns about potential disputes over risk allocation and financial exposure.

The agreement grants exclusive rights to BOSCH in the US and Canada for product distribution, limiting CLIENT's ability to compete. This raises concerns about potential market exclusivity and competitive disadvantage.

The agreement imposes significant indemnification obligations on CLIENT without clear definition of scope, potentially exposing them to substantial financial risk. The lack of clarity regarding the scope of indemnification raises concerns about potential disputes and unlimited financial exposure.

recommendations: Issue a formal request for clarification on the specific financial mechanics, risk allocation mechanisms, and indemnification provisions outlined in the agreement.
Demand that BOSCH provide detailed operational responsibilities and financial arrangements to ensure clarity regarding CLIENT's obligations and potential liabilities.
Notify Bosch of concerns regarding market exclusivity and competitive disadvantage arising from the agreement's terms.
confidenceNote: This document was analysed entirely on-device using a local model. No content was transmitted externally. For complex legal matters, verify findings with qualified counsel.
